plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-level ~20-minute lesson explaining what Machine Learning is, including a downloadable PPTX slide deck and a Python file containing explanations and example code.

subtasks.instruction: Design a beginner-friendly lesson structure (~20 minutes) on 'What is Machine Learning?' including learning objectives, pacing (minute-by-minute), key concepts (definition, supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement, train/test split, features/labels, overfitting, evaluation), and a simple real-world example to anchor the lesson. Provide a slide-by-slide outline with speaker notes.

Generate a PPTX slide deck for the lesson. Target 10–14 slides suitable for ~20 minutes. Include: title, objectives, what ML is (vs traditional programming), types of ML, key workflow (data->features->model->train->evaluate->deploy), example (e.g., house price prediction or spam detection), simple chart/diagram placeholders, common pitfalls (bias/overfitting/data leakage), and recap. Add concise speaker notes per slide. Export as a .pptx file named 'machine_learning_beginner_lesson.pptx'.

Create a well-commented Python file named 'ml_beginner_lesson.py' that doubles as lesson notes + runnable examples. It should explain concepts in comments/docstrings and include small, runnable demos using scikit-learn on a built-in dataset (e.g., Iris or Breast Cancer). Include: loading data, train/test split, a simple model (LinearRegression or LogisticRegression), training, predictions, evaluation metric, and a short section demonstrating overfitting idea (e.g., compare simple vs complex model or train vs test accuracy). Ensure code runs as a script with a main() and prints outputs. Avoid external data downloads beyond sklearn built-ins.
Provide a short README-style text in the output describing how to use the PPTX and run the Python script (requirements, run command). Ensure both artifacts are referenced and ready for download.

outline_lesson.instruction: The task output is null/empty, so none of the required semantic criteria can be satisfied. Provide a complete slide-by-slide lesson outline (~20 minutes) that explicitly includes: (1) minute-by-minute pacing totaling ~20 minutes (include the number '20' or a 0–20 min breakdown), (2) core topics including the word 'supervised' (also cover unsupervised, reinforcement, train/test split, features/labels, overfitting, evaluation), and (3) a slide list labeled with 'Slide' for each slide plus speaker notes and a simple real-world anchoring example.

write_python_lesson.instruction: Deterministic failure: required Python file artifact is missing (ml_beginner_lesson.py not found), so output cannot be evaluated. To fix: create and include an artifact at path exactly 'ml_beginner_lesson.py'. Ensure the script contains a main() guard and runnable sklearn example using a built-in dataset. Include explicit use of train_test_split, model training, predictions, and evaluation (e.g., accuracy_score). Add a short overfitting demo comparing train vs test performance (e.g., high-degree polynomial features or different model complexities).

build_pptx.instruction: Deterministic failure: the required PPTX artifact does not exist (machine_learning_beginner_lesson.pptx). Generate and export a .pptx file named exactly 'machine_learning_beginner_lesson.pptx' containing 10–14 slides. Include required sections: title, objectives, ML vs traditional programming, types of ML, workflow (data→features→model→train→evaluate→deploy), an example (house prices or spam), diagram/chart placeholders, common pitfalls (bias/overfitting/data leakage), recap, and concise speaker notes per slide. Ensure the result references the artifact path and indicates slide count.

package_delivery.instruction: Provide a README-style text that explicitly references the PPTX file name "machine_learning_beginner_lesson.pptx" and the Python script "ml_beginner_lesson.py", and include a clear run command that contains "python" (e.g., `python ml_beginner_lesson.py`). Mention any requirements (e.g., Python version, pip install commands) and indicate both artifacts are ready for download.

tasks.instruction: Design a beginner-friendly lesson structure (~20 minutes) on 'What is Machine Learning?' including learning objectives, pacing (minute-by-minute), key concepts (definition, supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement, train/test split, features/labels, overfitting, evaluation), and a simple real-world example to anchor the lesson. Provide a slide-by-slide outline with speaker notes.
